extraction.one_line_summary: POGACAR attacked solo with 50km remaining on gravel sectors after early crash, held 90+ seconds to finish despite ALAPHILIPPE and ASGREEN chasing.
tactical_patterns: POGACAR attacked with 50km out when ALAPHILIPPE was exhausted from crash-chasing, exploitingWorldChamp's depleted state tactically.
Quick-Step burned ALAPHILIPPE domestiques getting him back after crash, leaving him isolated when POGACAR countered the reunion.
ASGREEN led chase on penultimate gravel sector but waited too long; attacks need 60km+ buffer before final sectors.

rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: survived early crash then soloed 50km, proving GT climbers can dominate Strade when classics stars are weakened
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: crash at 100km forced chase effort that left him empty when race exploded; couldn't respond to POGACAR at 50km
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: neutral: strongest chaser on final sectors but lacked explosive power to close 90-second gap despite VALVERDE cooperation
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: career-best second at age 41 suggests experience reading gravel positioning trumps raw power on final sectors
rider_signals.RODRIGUEZ Carlos: negative: briefly followed POGACAR but drifted back before 25km mark, showing junior GT climbers lack Strade-specific resistance
